codeReview.summary: The post-round-4 fix materially improves ownership and adds new screenshots plus a dedicated flow file in commit `e65ee13`, but it still does not satisfy the feature's required proof shape. The new artifacts prove that Mission session `69792bf9-59b0-4933-a873-43c546853bc1` exists, was previously killed by user, is now paused, and remains inspectable/resumable. They do not prove one live same-session Electron Mission run traversed the Kill Worker action end to end. The flow still reselects an already-killed session and uses session/missionDir files as the substantive transition evidence, and one of the new setup screenshots is misdescribed: `kill-proof-after-askuser-submit.png` shows `Standard Chat` selected rather than `Mission`. The previous blockers are therefore not cleared.

issues.description: The flow claims `kill-proof-after-askuser-submit.png` shows Mission mode selected with model `CCH-GPT-5.4` visible, but the checked-in screenshot shows `Standard Chat` selected and `Mission` unselected. That means the fresh feature-owned setup capture does not actually prove a Mission-surface start for this supposed same-session kill-worker flow. The owner bundle repeats the same incorrect claim at `.factory/validation/mission-verification/mission-cross-flow-kill-worker-pause-proof.json:40`.

The new proof still does not show a live Kill Worker action on one Electron Mission session traversing `droid.kill_worker_session` / `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user") -> mission_paused` end to end. Its own resolution/summary say the feature reuses fresh namespace screenshots and then selects the already-killed Mission session `69792bf9-59b0-4933-a873-43c546853bc1`, tying post-kill UI to `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/...`, `progress_log.jsonl`, and `state.json`. Per `.factory/library/user-testing.md:73`, restored-session reselection and disk logs are supporting context only for VAL-CROSS-005; the proof must still show one live Electron Mission session traversing the destructive transition end to end. The current screenshot chain only shows post-kill states (`kill-proof-after-reload.png`, `kill-proof-mission-control.png`, `kill-proof-chat-resumable.png`), so the critical live kill transition remains unproven.

summary: Scrutiny still fails. Commit `e65ee13` adds a dedicated VAL-CROSS-005 flow file and screenshot bundle, and those artifacts do show a real paused/resumable killed Mission session. But the proof is still not fresh live same-session Mission-GUI-TEST Kill Worker evidence: it misstates the setup screenshot and relies on reselecting an already-killed session plus disk corroboration for the actual destructive transition.
